expected_properties: When FULL is asserted, a write attempt (RW=0, EN=1) does not change dataOut or internal memory
FULL remains asserted and EMPTY remains deasserted after the illegal write
additional_cases.goal: Verify that write operations are ignored when the buffer is full
notes: Observes that no state transition occurs despite stimulus
Conservative timing: wait one clock after stimulus to check stability
preconditions: Buffer is filled to capacity (FULL = 1) before stimulus
stimulus_intent: Apply a write request while the buffer reports FULL
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible

expected_properties: When EMPTY is asserted, a read attempt (RW=1, EN=1) does not change dataOut
EMPTY remains asserted and FULL remains deasserted after the illegal read
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that read operations are ignored when the buffer is empty
notes: Ensures no spurious data appears on dataOut
Uses one clock cycle observation after stimulus
stimulus_intent: Issue a read request while the buffer reports EMPTY
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible

expected_properties: Data popped follows reverse order of pushes (LIFO)
Flags update correctly throughout the sequence
additional_cases.goal: Demonstrate correct LIFO ordering across a sequence of pushes and pops
notes: Applies a series of writes to fill the buffer, then reads back all entries
Observes dataOut sequence matches reverse of dataIn sequence
preconditions: Buffer is in reset state (Rst=1) before starting sequence
stimulus_intent: Push four distinct values, then pop them all, checking output order
timing_assumptions: Do not assume fixed latency; verify output after each read cycle

expected_properties: EMPTY deasserts after a successful write when buffer transitions from empty to non‑empty
FULL asserts after the fourth successful write
READ operation restores EMPTY when last element is popped
dataOut reflects the most recently written value after a read
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate correct flag behavior and dataOut values for both push and pop operations
notes: Enriches basic case to cover both write and subsequent read in a single stimulus sequence
stimulus_intent: Perform a write (RW=0, EN=1) with a known dataIn value, then a read (RW=1, EN=1) to verify LIFO behavior
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible
